def: s.f. A bow; the lock of a gun; the stocks; the rack
more: n. f. S. चाप 1. A bow. 2. The lock of a gun. 3. Michelia kisopa , Wat. chāṅp chaṛhānā , v. n. 1. To cock a gun; to bend the bow. Shām gāt, sir jaṭā baṛhāë, Arun nain, sar chāṅp chaṛhāë . Rāmāyan. 2. To punish or torture by squeezing the ear under the lock of a gun; to torture.
s. f. 1. The lock of a gun. 2. The stocks, the rack. چانپ چڙهانا chāmp chaṛhānā, 1. To cock a gun. 2. To punish or torment by squeezing the ear with the lock of a gun.
subs: (-kī), To bend a bow; to cock a gun; — to punish or torture by squeezing the ear under the lock of a gun; to put in the stocks.

def: s.m. Uncooked rice ( = ćāwal, q.v.) s.m. Rice cleared of the husk and not cooked (when boiled it is termed bhāt); — the weight of a grain of rice, one-eighth of a rattī, q.v.
more: n. m. S. चण्डुल 1. Rice separated from the husk. 2. One eighth of a rattī , q. v. chāṅval chabvānā , v. a. To cause persons suspected of theft to chew uncooked rice, a deficiency of saliva being held to indicate the thief.
subs: (-se), To make (one) chew rice (over which an incantation has been pronounced: this is done in the case of persons suspected of stealing, it being believed that the thief will be discovered by blood issuing from his mouth, or by a deficiency of saliva being produced.

def: s.m. A small species of millet.
s.m. The Bos grunniens, &c. ( = ćamar, q.v.); — the male flower of maize
more: n. m. S. चमर a chaurī , Pr. chamaram, chāmaram . 1. The tail of the Bos grunniens; the yak; a flapper to shake off flies, etc; a sign of royalty. Bāloṅ bāṅdhī ek chhināl, Nit voh rahtī khole bāl, Dhanī ko chhoṛ chākar se rājī, Chatrā ho so arth bichārī . Riddle. Ans. Chauṅrī . A fly flapper. A female frail, with hairs she's strung, All night and day her hair loose hung, Her spouse she leaves, to servants clung— Who wit possess can give her tongue. 2. The male flower of maize (E. dhan bāl ). 3. E. A field. chaṅvar ḍholnā , v. a. To drive off flies with a chaurī .
s. m. The tail of the Bos grunniens, a species of ox, used to whisk off flies, &c. See چونري chauṅrī.
subs: To shake a ćaṅwar or whisk (for the purpose of driving away flies, &c.), to whisk off flies with a ćaurī.

n. f. S. चुप Silence ( khāmoshī ); stillness. 1. Ek chup, sau ko harāë . Prov. One man who keeps silent will defeat a hundred who talk. 2. Sab se bhalī chup . Prov. Silence is best. 3. Chup kī dād Khudā degā . Prov. God will avenge the wrongs in silence borne. 4. Bārū jaise kirkirī, ujjal jaise dhup, Aisī mīṭhī kuchh nahīṅ jaisī mīṭhī chup . Dohā. As crisp as sand and as the sun full bright, But nothing is so sweet as silence quite. 5. Chup ādhī marzī . Prov. Silence is half-consent. chup, chup-rah, choṅch band kar ; P. khāmosh , intj. Hold your tongue! silence! peace! chup chāp, chup chupāte , adv. Silently; quite still; stealthily; secretly; peaceably. chup chup , adj. Silent; mute; dumb. Phr. Still as a mouse; still as a statue. chup k. , v. a. 1. To preserve silence; to stop any noise. 2. To confute; silence; shut up. chupke bāham salāh k . To consult or conspire together in secret; …
adj. 1. Silent. 2. interj. Silence! (the word ره being understood). 3. s. f. Silence: as, چپ لگني يا چپکي لگني chup lagnī or chupkī lagnī, v. n. To be struck dumb.
adv. (dakh.) Gratis, for nothing, uselessly ( Harris ).
subs: To run zigzag when pursued by an enemy (in order to baffle him), to dodge
adj., adv. & s.m. Right and left; — carelessness; unsteadiness, want of principle.

def: s.m. The refuse of the jhaṛ-berī after the pālā is beaten from it
subs: s.m. Gross measurement of the lands of an estate.

def: s.f. = ćabak, ṭapak, qq.v. s.f. Throbbing, throb, shoot of pain ( = ṭabhak; ṭapak, qq.v.). s.f. Sound of (water, &c.) dropping; dropping; beat, throb, pulsation, &c. ( = ṭapak, q.v.).
more: n. So called from its note: The goatsucker; caprimulgus . They breed on the ground, making no nest and often lay in a rut. chip'pa/?/kh , adj. Thin; sunken. Biyāh hote hī gāl chippakh ho gaë . No sooner wed than his cheeks all sunken grew.
adj. 1. Shallow. 2. s. f. Name of a bird.

ety: ćapak˚ = Prk. चपक्क(इ) or चपक्के(इ), fr. S. चप or चर्प+कृ
def: v.n. To be compressed; to be pressed flat down or on; to collapse; to stick, adhere; to cling (to); — to take up (with), form an unlawful connexion (with), to be entangled (in an amour), to fall in love (with); to spread or sink (as ink on damp paper)
more: v. a. 1. To paste; gum; to stick on. 2. To put up or affix (a notice). 3. To provide for; get one a berth.
v. n. 1. To stick; adhere. 2. See ulajhnā , 9, 10. chipakvānā , Caus. of chipkānā , q. v.

v. n. 1. To stick, to adhere. 2. To spread or sink (as, ink on damp paper). 3. To be compressed. چپک جانا chipak jānā, v. a. 1. To be sold on account of its sweetness. 2. To fall in love at first sight.
subs: v.n. (intens.) To stick close (to), to stick together; to be compressed, &c. = ćipaknā; — to be sold on account of (its) sweetness (cf. pićaknā, and ćipaṭnā).

def: s.m. Fickleness, unsteadiness; mobility, quick movement, quickness, swiftness.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā) & adv. Shaking, trembling, tremulous, unsteady, agitated, restless, wavering; variable, inconstant, fickle, uncertain, volatile, giddy, wanton; active, quick, swift, agile, expeditious; swiftly, quickly, &c.; — an unsteady person, &c.
more: adj. S. चपल 1. Quick; brisk; glancing; vivid; brilliant. 2. Wanton ( achpal , 3.). Komal gāt, umar hai bālī, chaṅdrmukhī, chaplā, chanchal . Song. Lāonī . As velvet soft, youthful, moon-faced, mincing and skittish too.
adj. 1. Tremulous, shaking. 2. Wanton, restless, volatile.
subs: adj. Of a fickle or inconstant nature

v.t. To cause (one) to feel shame, to abash; — to place one thing on another, to heap, pile; to press down, compress, flatten, squeeze, crush.
more: v. a. To cause to feel shame. Jab roṭiyoṅ ke ba'ne kā ā-kar paṭe shumār, Muflis ko ek deṅ, to tavaṅggar ko ch/?/ār chār, Gar aur māṅge voh to use jhiṛkeṅ bār bār, Is muflisī kā āh! bayāṅ kyā karūṅ maiṅ, yār? Muflis ko us jagah bhī chapātī hai muflisī . On poverty. Nazīr. When meal-cakes served out are at times in charity, For one the poor man gets the rich gets over three, If more he asks for with harsh speech browbeat is he, Alas! what words can tell the tale of misery, Even here the poor are brought to shame by poverty.
v. a. 1. To abash. 2. To place one thing on another, to pile.

more: n. f. S. चर्पटी A thin cake of flour and water, without leaven, slightly baked or toasted over an open fire. 1. Paisā paisā bhar kī chapātī pakātī hai . She bakes thin cakes but one pice weight. 2. ' Tīn chapātī, nau barātī, khāo chūram chūr!' 'Aë ghar-basī! tere biyāh hai, yā lūṭam lūṭ!' 'Bandī jab kartī hai, aisā hī kartī hai .' Wom. Prov. Hostess . 'Three wheaten cakes, nine wedding guests—feast, feast my friends, nor spare.' Neighbour . 'Destroyer of thy house! no marriage feast but plunder rare!' Hostess . 'Your servants' feasts are all like this —a sumptuous affair.' chapātī sā peṭ , A soft stomach.
s. f. A thin cake of unleavened bread.

def: v.n. To be compressed, be flattened; to be crushed or squeezed; — to be abashed or ashamed; to be bashful; to blush; to lower the eyes, to cower, quail; to submit, stoop; to be put to silence.
more: v. n. S. चप to be quiet. 1. To be silent. 2. To be ashamed, abashed, bashful; to blush. 3. To hang down one's eyes; to blink; cower; quail ( jhepnā ). Jo chap chap-kar āṅkh jhapāve, O ke ran māṅ sel chalāve.? Rus. Prov. Who blink and turn their eyes away with spear in battle ne'er make way. 4. To be shut up; hushed.
v. n. 1. To be abashed, to be bashful in company, to blush, submit, stoop. 2. To be crushed or squeezed. ¶ chipnā (for چهپنا chhipnā, q. v.) v. n. To be concealed.

def: s.f. A flat lid or cover (of a cooking pot; syn. sar-posh; cf. ḍhaknī); a saucer; — the cap of the knee, knee-pan; — the crown of the head
more: 1. A cover ( ḍhaknā ); saucer. Likh-kar chapnī sir par dharī Nikal paṛā yā nikal paṛī . Mah. A charm. An earthen plate writ on and laid upon her head, Delivered of a boy or girl the brought-to-bed. ☞ The legend is that a holy man, called Shaikh Farīd-ud-dīn, had the miraculous power of turning stone into sugar. Hence, he was surnamed Shakar-ganj. The above couplet refers to the custom of inscribing his name, with the above couplet, on an earthen plate and placing it as a charm on the head of the lying-in-woman to facilitate delivery. 2. The knee-pan; knee-cap. 3. The crown of the head. chapnī bhar pānī meṅ ḍūb marnā , v. n. 1. To drown oneself in a spoonful of water. 2. To be greatly abashed; to be ready to die with shame.

s. f. A lid, a cover, top (small). چپني بهرپاني مين يا ليکي ڐوب مرنا chapnī bhar panī meṅ (or leke) ḍūb marnā (lit. to drown oneʼs self in a saucer-full of water) To be greatly abashed. چپني چاٿني chapnī chāṭnī (lit. to lick the pot-lid) To be contented with little.
s. f. Cap (of the knee: Harris ).
subs: (or pānī leke) ḍub-marnā, 'To drown oneself in a saucer-full of water'; to be greatly abashed
'To lick the pot-lid'; to be contented with little.

def: s.m. One who wears a ćaprās or badge, a peon, orderly, beadle, messenger (of a court, &c.)
more: n. m. A peon; orderly; beadle; messenger of a court. chaprāsī-i-adālat . A bailiff; a process server.
s. m. A messenger or other servant wearing a chaprās.

def: s.m. = H چپڙاس चपड़ास ćapṛās, s.f. See ćaprās, and ćaprāsī.
adj. Brazen-faced, shameless, bold, impudent; — s.m. A slipper.
adv. Quickly, instantly, forthwith.
more: adj. Oiled; oily; greasy; rich. Chupṛī, aur do do! Prov. Buttered, and two (cakes)! (Dainties and plenty). (Said usually of offices in which authority is combined with a large salary). chupṛī bāt , n. f. Flattery; soft or honeyed words. chupṛī roṛī , n. f. Cakes rubbed over with clarified butter.

Mār. n. f. Cakes of cow-dung ((اُپلا)). Chhao ganḍā ke chipṛī lihalā se kā hokhī? Chār man ke lakṛī ne jarāib, je koilo hokhī! Bhoj. What is the good of three annas worth of cow-pats? Burn four maunds of wood and you have the charcoal in the bargain.

def: s.f. A puddle; — a small species of pulse (resembling ćanā); — husk, bran (cf. ćapṛā).
s.f. A cake of cow-dung.
more: adj. f. 1. Oiled, greasy. 2. Smooth, plausible. چپڙي بات chupṛī-bāt, Flattery, soft words. چپڙي روٿي chupṛī-roṭī, Cakes rich with clarified butter.

(trans. of چپٿنا chapaṭnā ) v. a. To flatten.

more: s. f. The name of an insect.

ety: Prk. चत्तारि = S.चत्वारि, neut. pl. of चतुर्; — the Pers. ćār is a contrac. of ćahār, q.v.
def: adj. Four
more: adj. S. चत्वार, Z. chathware , P. chahār . Four. 1. Laṛākā ke chār kān . Prov. The pugnacious man has four ears. (He hears twice as much as a peaceable man (to make a quarrel of). 2. Jiske chār bhaiyā, māreṅ dhaul, chhin leṅ rupaiyā . Prov. He who has four brothers may knock you down and take your money. 3. Chār hāth pāoṅ sab ke haiṅ . Prov. Saying. Every one has two arms and two legs. (Two can play at the same game). chār abrū kā safāyā , Mah. n. m. Shaving of the eyebrows and whiskers; and generally, shaving of the head, beard, eyebrows, and moustaches. chār ādmī, lit . four men. People in general; a jury of arbitrators. Jo chār ādmī kaheṅ so manzūr . chār-uṅgal, chavvā , n. m. A measure equal to four fingers' breadth; one girah , q. v. or 2½ inches. chār-āīnā , n. m. Armour. chār band , n. Joints; limbs. Kyoṅ yāro, is makāṅ meṅ ye kaisī chalī havā, Jo muflisī se hosh kisī kā nahīṅ baj…

adj. Four. چارآبرو chār-ābrū, s. m. A kind of dervis who shaves his eyebrows and whiskers. چارآنکهين chār-ānkheṅ (four eyes) s. f. See چارچشم chār-chashm. چارانگل chār-ungal, s. m. A palm (four inches). چارانگلي chār-unglī, s. f. Handʼs breadth. چارآينه chār-āʼīna, s. m. Iron armour. چاربالش chār-bālish, s. m. A throne, couch, or sofa. چاربانگ chār-bāng, adj. Sensible, quick, intelligent, alert. چاربيسي chār-bīsī, s. Fourscore. چارپا chār-pā, s. A quadruped: see چارپايه chār-pāya. چارپاره chār-pāra, adj. Divided into four, four-pieced. چارٿوک chār-ṭūk, adj. Broken (into four pieces). چارجامه chār-jāma, s. m. A kind of saddle (without a tree). چارچشم chār-chashm, s. f. Meeting, interview, the eyes of two people meeting. چارچند chār-chand, adj. Quadruple, fourfold. چارخانه chār-ḵẖāna, 1. adj. Chequered. 2. s. m. A kind of cloth. چارديواري chār-dīwār-ī, s. f. A court-yard, enclosure, area. …
s. (dakh.) A kind of dish or soup, mulligatawny ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.m. Food (for cattle), fodder, forage, herbage; provisions; bait (for fish); a young plant
more: n. m. S. चर to eat. 1. Forage; fodder; bait for fish. 2. Provisions. Bahin kahe 'merā bīr pyārā,' Kāl kahe 'merā hai yeh chārā .' Kabīr. The sister says 'my brother own is he.' Says Death, ''tis he I've marked my prey to be.' chār'ū , n. m. A great eater. Chārū so bhārū . Prov. Who eats bears (burdens). narm chārā , Farinaceous or soft food; slops.
s. m. Remedy, &c. See P. and H. چاره chāra.

def: s.m. Remedy, &c. = P. ćāra, q.v.
s.m. = چارا ćārā, q.v. s.m. Food (for cattle), fodder, forage, herbage; provisions; bait (for fish); a young plant
s.m. Grazing, pasturing; pasture
more: n. m. chiṛī , n. f. the hen sparrow. S. चटक 1. A cock sparrow. Chiṛā lāyā dāl kā dānā, chiṛyā lāī chāval kā dānā, dono ne mil-ke khichṛī pakāī . Kahānī. 2. The blue throats and calliope. Cyaneula suecica and calliope pectoralis , birds of the chat family found in the hills. They are brown, with bright colored throats, red or blue —winter visitors to the plains, retiring to the hills to breed.
subs: s.f. Grazing-ground; grazing-land, pasture; a meadow.

def: v.t. To perfume (the body) by smearing or plastering it with an unguent made of sandal-wood, to anoint; to worship.

v. a. To consider, to reflect, to apprehend, to conceive.

def: s.m. A lamp; a light
more: n. m. 1. A lamp. 1. Chirāg raushan, murād hāsil! Prov. Thy lamp place on the shrine, answered is prayer thine. 2. Ghar meṅ chirāg tak nahīṅ jaltā! Not even a light in his house! (He is extremely poor. Or, he is so stingy!). 3. Jis ghar meṅ chālīs din chirāg na jale bhūt kā dakhal ho jātā hai . Wom. Evil spirits enter the house in which no light has been burned for forty days. 2. ( Met .). The light of the house. A son. Ghar kā chirāg. chirāg battī, lit . a lamp and a wick. Lights. chirāg battī k. , v. a. To prepare the lamps for lighting. chirāg battī kā waqt , n. m. Candle light; evening. chirāg baṛhānā, bujhānā, ṭhanḍā, gul, yā khāmosh k., chirāg ko hāth denā , v. n. To put out the light. chirāg-pā , adj. 1. Rearing (a horse). 2. Turned upside down. Auṅdhe muṅh, chirāg-pā . Prov. The horse on his hind legs, the rider on his back. chirāg-pā h. , v. n. 1. def: v.t. (dialec.) = ćaṛhānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to ascend, or mount, or embark, &c.; to cause to rise, or increase, or swell, &c.; to raise, lift, take up; to advance, elevate, exalt, magnify; to offer up (oblations or sacrifice), to sacrifice, devote; to brace (a drum, &c.); to string (a bow); to bend; to pull, draw on or up; to cock; to put (on, upon, or into, -par, — as a utensil on a fire, or colour on a thing, or goods into a boat or ship, &c.); to spread, to apply (colour, or decorations, to, -par, cloth, &c.); to fix (a bayonet, &c.); to put in hand (a work); to raise to the mouth, to drink, imbibe; to lead (an army, against, -par)
more: (trans. of چڙنا) v. a. To vex, irritate, provoke, offend, mock, gibe, jeer.
v. a. (dakh.) See چڙهانا chaṛhānā. ¶ chuṛānā (dakh.) To make grimaces ( Harris ).

def: v.t. To tend grazing cattle, to graze, pasture; to feed; — to have (one) under command; to lead (one by the nose); to make a fool of.
more: v. a. P. charīdan , चर to eat. 1. To graze; pasture; feed. 1. Dadhī, mākhan ke chor ho, kahte bāt banāë, Sab chaturāī chhoṛ-ke, jāo charāo gāe! Krishn-līlā. You steal our butter and milk, and gammon with smooth phrase, Leave off your wily tricks, go to, your cattle graze! 2. Ab ḍhoroṅ nūṅ kaun charāve? Lalvā mūl na jāvegā, Ḍhor raheṅ saë din din bhūke, niyār kaṛe se āvegā? Rus. song. No one to graze the cattle now, this boy wont take them out, The tired cattle starve each day, no fodder to be got. 2. To mix up ( milānā ). Ṭeṭoṅ meṅ non charā do . 3. To command; lead by the nose. Woh aisoṅ ko charāë phirtā hai . 4. To make a fool of ( ahmaq banānā ).

v. a. S. चुर to steal; Pālī, choretī . 1. To steal; filch; cabbage; commit theft. 1. Guā'in se Krishn jī kaheṅ madhurī boli meṅ, 'E, kahā churāë jāt ho tum cholī meṅ? ' Thus Krishn to the milkmaids spoke in honeyed accents sweet, 'Some thing you've stolen and hid within your bosom's safe retreat?' 2. Bājū-band bhūli palaṅg par, pāve tau denā, mahāra, Sās morī dūtī, nanad morī bai ān, sautan līyo hai churāë . Song. Ṭhumrī . Give me, my lord, my armlet on the bed forgotten left, wife keen for theft. Your mother a spy, your sister my foe, my co- 3. Hāth lakaṛyā, kāṅdhe kamalyā, ban meṅ gauveṅ charāve . Song. Kanhaiyā. 2. To embezzle; misappropriate. 3. To absorb; suck in; take in; swallow. Pānī churānā . 4. To commit plagiary; to plagiarize. Jo churāte haiṅ mere mazmūn-i-nau ijād ko, Khānā-dāmād apnā karte haiṅ merī aulād ko .
v. n. 1. To crackle before breaking. 2. To throb; smart. Zakhm charrāyā jātā hai . 3. To cause to tear or to be torn. 4. To emit the sound made in tearing cloth.
( चारण or caus. of چرنا charnā ) v. a. To graze, to pasture, to feed.
v. a. To steal, filch, rob.
(causal of چيرنا) v. a. To cause to tear or be torn, cause to split.
v. n. 1. To burst. 2. To ache, to smart.

def: v.n. To go up, ascend, mount, climb; to soar; to ride (on, -par); to go (on board), embark; to go, be taken or conveyed, be transported (to); to go (on), fit; to go forward, advance, proceed (as a procession); to come on (as fever, &c.); to rise (as a price, a river, &c.), to increase, swell; to accumulate, fall in arrears (as pay. &c.); to be enlarged, to spread, to grow; to be raised, elevated, exalted, promoted; to be offered (as a sacrifice); to be drawn on or up, be tucked up; to be strung (as a bow); to be braced (as a drum, &c.); to be fixed (as a bayonet, or an arrow); to be set (as a bone); to be put (on, or in, as colour; or a weight, &c. in a scale, &c.), to be applied, to be spread or laid (over); to be entered (in, as an account or item), be put down; to march (against), lead an attack (against); to invade; to enter (the mind); to enter (the system, or head, &c.) and take effect (as poison, intoxicants, fever, &c.); to be possessed (by an evil spirit); to be taken, be caught, be hooked, be entrapped; to be superior (to), to excel; to gain (as a watch); to pass, elapse (as time, e.g. pahar-bhar din ćaṛhā); to be put in hand, be taken in hand (as a work)
more: v. n. 1. To ascend, mount, advance, attack, embark, board, rise, climb, soar, spread, swell, ride. 2. To be strung (a bow), to be braced (a drum). 3. To be offered up (an oblation or sacrifice). چڙهنا اترنا chaṛhnā utarnā, v. n. 1. To hire and dismiss. 2. Congressus.

def: s.m. A wheel (as of a water-mill, or of a well, &c.); a potter's wheel; a lathe; the celestial globe or orb, the sphere of the heavens, the heavens, the sky; — circular motion; turn; — fortune, chance; — a cross-bow; a bow in a very tense state; — a falcon (particularly a white one); a hyena
more: n. m. S. चक्र 1. The celestial globe; heavenly sphere; the sky; the heavens. 2. A lathe. 3. A hawk. 4. A hyæna. charkh-pūjā , Cor. of chakr-pūjā . A ceremony observed by the lower orders of Hindus in Bengal, on the day when the sun enters Aries, for the expiation of their sins. The performer is suspended by an iron hook passed through the skin of the back, to one end of the lever, which is raised on the top of a high pole and whirled round by means of a rope fixed to the other end. This penance is performed for rich people by deputy. charkh chaṛhānā , v. a. To turn on a lathe. charkh chaṛhnā , v. n. To be turned (as in a lathe). charkh khānā , v. n. 1. ( chakrānā ) To turn round; circulate; revolve. 2. To be formed into globules (as quicksilver). charkh hiṅḍolā . A merry-go-round.

s. m. 1. A wheel (particularly, a potterʼs, or of a watermill, a well, or an oil-press). 2. The sky, the heavens, the celestial globe, the sphere. 3. Circular motion. 4. Turn, press. 5. Fortune, chance. 6. An hyena. جرخ اثير charḵẖ-i-as̤īr, The heaven of the stars. چرخ اطلس charḵẖ-i-at̤las, The crystalline sphere or heaven. چرخ پوجا charḵẖ-pūjā, A ceremony observed by the lower orders of Hindūs in Bengal, on the day when the sun enters Aries, for the expiation of their sins. They are suspended, by an iron hook passed through the skin of the back, to one end of a lever, which is raised on the top of a high pole, and whirled round by means of a rope fixed to the other end. چرخ چڙهنا charḵẖ chaṛhnā, v. n. 1. To dress or adorn oneʼs self. 2. To turn (as, in a lathe). چرخ دوار charḵẖ-i-dauwār, s. m. The sky, the heavens, the firmament. چرخ دولابي charḵẖ-i-dolābī, s. The sky, the celestial orb…

more: n. m. 1. A spinning wheel; distaff; reel. 1. Lūṭ meṅ char/?/khā nafā . Prov. Whate'er as booty you obtain, be it a distaff even it's gain. 2. Paisā hī raṅg rūp hai, paisā hī māl hai, Paisā na ho to ādmī charkhe kī māl hai . Nazīr. 'Tis coin enjoyment makes, coin is the only property, Coinless man as the distaff string vain turns round constantly. 3. Jab kubṛī kamar ho gaī, aur sar huā gālā, Muṅh sūkh-ke chamrakh huā, aur tan huā taklā, Phir roṭī ko charkhe se kamā khātī hai buṛhyā . Nazir. Her body bent with age, a hump-back quite, her hair snow-white, Shrunk face and wrinkled skin, her body as a spindle thin, In old age taken to spinning now her bread to get somehow. 4. Uṭhāo charkhā, būṅdeṅ āiṅ . Bring in the spinning wheel, it rains. 2. An old creaking worn-out cart. 3. Met . The body char/?/khā ponī, yā pānī , Wom. lit . spinning and carding.
